title: Hold the onions – and see if they make you cry
summary: Feedback isn't sure what to make of a ground-breaking piece of research into the understudied topic of "subjective individual variability in onion tearing and its relationship to chemosensory sensitivity"

title: The 17 best popular science books of 2026 so far
summary: The first six months of the year have brought us popular science reads on everything from consciousness to cosmology. Liz Else rounds up her favourites

title: SpaceX's secretive plans to deliver cargo to Earth from space
summary: Not much is known about Starfall, SpaceX's new delivery system, but an assessment published in May revealed its intended purpose

title: 600 مليار دولار خسائر القيمة السوقية لـ SpaceX في 3 جلسات
summary: تراجعت أسهم "سبيس إكس" لليوم الثالث على التوالي، متكبدة خسائر بمئات المليارات من الدولارات في قيمتها السوقية، وذلك بعدما أعلنت الشركة لأول مرة عن طرح سندات استثمارية عالية الجودة.

title: Walking shark found in Papua New Guinea is new to science
summary: Hemiscyllium dudgeonae is the tenth recorded species of walking shark, which use their pectoral fins to move across reef flats, and its limited range means it may be at high risk of extinction

title: The relationship recession is even bigger for Gen Z than we thought
summary: We know that members of Gen Z are less likely to be in a steady relationship than millennials were at their age, but previous research missed out an important factor that actually widens the relationship recession

title: Millions of fossil whale bones found in deep-ocean ‘necropolis’
summary: Researchers diving 7 kilometres deep in a crewed submersible have discovered a vast collection of whale bones, including fossils up to 5 million years old and species new to science

title: Why we should all take quantum physics extremely personally
summary: Physics is considered a cold, hard science – but it will transform your life if you view it with a bit more subjectivity, says Karmela Padavic-Callaghan

title: معرض تونس الدولي للكتاب: “تيك توك” و”أنستغرام” يقودان اليافعين إلى أجنحة دور النشر واستياء من قلة الكتب بالإنقليزية
summary: تونس 28 أفريل 2026 (وات) – يشهد معرض تونس الدولي للكتاب في دورته الأربعين من 23 أفريل إلى 3 ماي 2026، إقبالا هاما طغت عليه مشاهد الأولياء رفقة أبنائهم ممن يزورون المعرض بحثا عن عناوين يتطلعون إلى قراءتها ولاكتشاف جديد دور النشر، فضلا عن ملاقاة كُتابهم المفضلين. وأثناء تجوله في المعرض يمكن للزائر أن…
